title: outpace doc — polish note about responsiveness and haptics
html:
outpace doc — polish note about responsiveness and haptics

Source: 2026-03-04 Google Doc. Tiny in-progress polish note about Outpace iOS feel. Belongs to the stealth-apps cluster in active build mode.

TL;DR

  • The complaint: typing-name responsiveness is laggy — "the responsiveness and snappiness that we need from an Apple App Award winner is not there."
  • Specific ask: stronger haptics throughout the app, especially during onboarding.
  • The bar: explicitly Apple Design Award level — Yahir is benchmarking Outpace's tactile feel against the polish ceiling, not against shipping competitors.

Pattern observed

Tiny doc but reveals Yahir's design quality bar for Outpace: not "good enough to launch" but "good enough to win Apple's Design Award." Same perfectionist standard he applies to onboarding flows in IRL Lore and Swipr. The onboarding-haptics emphasis specifically suggests Yahir believes the first-90-seconds tactile experience is the differentiator.

This note likely became a Claude Code task or Cursor prompt — the casual "we" framing suggests dictation to an AI agent.

Key quote

"the responsiveness and snappiness that we need from an Apple App Award winner is not there."

Open questions

  • Was this actually fixed in a build? (No follow-up doc found yet.)
  • Is the Outpace iOS app shipping or still pre-launch as of March 2026?
